items.title: Bas van Fraassen - Can Moral Theory Affect Deep Ontology?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

Normally, if we have a belief system, such as a particular religion, some kind of morality follows. Can the direction of causation go in reverse? If we commit to a particular morality, whether absolute or relative, are there implications for ultimate reality? Can morality say anything about the deep nature of the world?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Bastiaan Cornelis "Bas" van Fraassen is a Dutch-American philosopher noted for his contributions to philosophy of science, epistemology and formal logic. He is a Distinguished Professor of Philosophy at San Francisco State University and the McCosh Professor of Philosophy Emeritus at Princeton University.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

items.title: Derek Turner - Evolution and Religion?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

Two kinds of questions describe the relationship between evolution and religion. The first explores discrepancies, even contradictions, between the science of evolution and the beliefs of religion. The second examines how religion itself evolved.

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Derek Turner teaches philosophy at Connecticut College where he is also the Karla Heurich Harrison ’28 Director of the Goodwin-Niering Center for the Environment. Turner’s research focuses on philosophical issues in historical science, especially paleontology and the earth sciences.

Make a donation to Closer To Truth to help us continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

items.title: Fr. Philip Larrey - Dualism: Arguing Pro and Con
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/GSR4k

Dualism is the theory that ultimate reality is composed of two radically different kinds of things, physical things and non-physical things, and that these same two kinds of things, expressed as body/brain and spirit/soul, account for consciousness. What are the arguments for and against Dualism (like the ‘interaction problem’)?

Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Fr. Philip Larrey, Ph.D., is a Catholic priest and professor of philosophy at Boston College. He previously held the Chair of Logic and Epistemology at the Pontifical Lateran University in the Vatican, where he also served as Dean of the Philosophy Department. His scholarly work focuses on the philosophy of knowledge, critical thinking, and the impact of the digital era on society.

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

items.title: What are the physics of time travel?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Astrophysicist J. Richard Gott on the physics of time travel, Einstein, and the future of space exploration. #shorts</article>

items.title: Clément Vidal on The Noosphere: The Intelligent Global Brain | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Is Earth becoming a super-organism? Philosopher Clément Vidal shares his thoughts and discusses the noosphere, the idea that all human thought and knowledge together form a kind of “sphere” around the Earth. The noosphere is like an invisible layer made up of our collective mind, a realm of human thought, culture, science, art, and shared understanding.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

Dr. Clément Vidal is a philosopher with a background in logic and cognitive sciences. He is co-director of the 'Evo Devo Universe' community and founder of the 'High Energy Astrobiology' prize. To satisfy his intellectual curiosity when facing the big questions, he brings together many areas of knowledge such as cosmology, physics, astrobiology, complexity science, evolutionary theory, and philosophy of science.</article>

items.title: Dean Zimmerman - Is Evil Necessary in God's World?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

If we seek God, we cannot avoid the problem of evil. Can the enormity of evil ever be compatible with an all-powerful, all-knowing, all-good God? Couldn’t God have created the world without such evil? A little evil, perhaps, so that there are consequences of acts, but the unrelenting, monumental evil in the world exceeds all decency. Did God have no choice?

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Dean W. Zimmerman is an American professor of philosophy at Rutgers University specializing in metaphysics and philosophy of religion.

Closer To Truth is now on Bluesky! Follow us for updates, announcements, and more: https://shorturl.at/Zbk40

items.title: Elisabeth Lloyd - Philosophy of Sex, Sexuality, & Gender
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without paywalls: https://shorturl.at/l3q6G

Sexual questions abound. What is “normal” sexual behavior vs. multiple variations? How to appreciate LGBTQ+ issues? What can be learned from sex in animal models that is relevant for human sexuality? A philosophical perspective can provide clarity.

Watch more videos on this topic here: https://shorturl.at/v87Of

Elisabeth “Lisa” Anne Lloyd is an American philosopher of science specializing in the philosophy of biology. She is currently Distinguished Professor of History and Philosophy of Science and Medicine and Adjunct Professor of biology at Indiana University, Bloomington, affiliated faculty scholar at the Kinsey Institute for Research in Sex, Gender, and Reproduction and Adjunct Faculty at the Center for the Integrative Study of Animal Behavior.

items.title: Edi Bilimoria - Consciousness and Life After Death?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Watch more videos on this topic here: https://shorturl.at/lbF3K

Life after death is explored in the context of diverse theories of consciousness, from strict Materialism/Physicalism to those of ancient wisdom traditions. We explore the view that the haunting and deeply personal question of life after death relates to theories of consciousness.

Contribute what you can to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Edi Bilimoria was born in India and educated at the universities of London, Sussex and Oxford, he presents an unusual blend of experience in the fields of science, arts and philosophy. Professionally, Edi is an award-winning engineer.

items.title: Oludamini Ogunnaike - The Role of Art in Diverse Religions?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Almost all religions use art in their sacred spaces and many use art forms in their worship rituals. Certainly, there is powerful social cohesion at work. Can cross-religion communications in art, largely nonverbal, work to enhance similarities and mitigate differences among religions that, at least 
superficially, have significant differences? Can art be a much-needed unifier?

Make a donation of any amount to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions: https://shorturl.at/Xsn2z

Oludamini Ogunnaike is Associate Professor of African Religious Thought and Democracy at the University of Virginia (UVA).

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

items.title: Quayshawn Spencer - Can Evolutionary Biology Explain Ethics?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow Closer To Truth on X for daily articles, videos, updates, and more: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

Can biology—especially evolutionary biology—help explain ethics, and if so, can science influence ethics and help set new norms? If science can, should science do? Topics range from issues of sex/gender to punishment for crime.

Subscribe to our podcast on any podcast platform for new episodes every Wednesday: https://shorturl.at/BCxtj

Quayshawn Spencer is an assistant professor at the Department of Philosophy at University of San Francisco. He holds a BA in chemistry and an MA in biology from Cornell University. He earned his PhD and was a Ford Foundation postdoctoral fellow at Stanford University.

items.title: Greg Ganssle - Could God be a Person?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Shop Closer To Truth merchandise for items like mugs, T-shirts, hoodies, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

What would it mean for God to be more than one person? ‘Three persons in one God’ is the Christian claim, and in what sense can ‘one God consisting of three persons’ even make sense? What would be the theology of multiple persons as God? Why three persons in the Godhead? What’s the difference between polytheism, many gods, and multiple persons in the same God?

Watch more videos on this topic here: https://shorturl.at/Ym3bG

Greg Ganssle (PhD, Syracuse) is a professor of philosophy at Talbot School of Theology at Biola University. He is the author of several books, including "A Reasonable God: Engaging the New Face of Atheism" and "Thinking About God", and he is the editor of "God and Time".

items.title: Anjan Chatterjee - What Makes Art Transcendent?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

How can art, the visual arts—from painting to sculptures to film—take us beyond ourselves, generating a sense of awe and grandeur, opening up new ways of knowing and revealing new kinds of understanding? Why do only certain pieces of art, whether complex classical paintings or simple line drawings, trigger personal transcendence while most others do not? What is it about art that triggers the transcendent?

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Anjan Chatterjee is a professor of neurology at the Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania. He is director of the Penn Center for Neuroaesthetics and a member of the Center for Cognitive Neuroscience. His research focuses on spatial cognition and its relationship to language.

Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you listen: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

items.title: Zorana Pringle - Aspects of Consciousness?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Make a donation to help Closer To Truth continue exploring the world's deepest questions without the need for paywalls: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

We offer distinctive approaches to consciousness including cognitive science, phenomenology, and even the implications of aesthetics like fiction and films. We observe the richness of consciousness studies.

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Zorana Ivcevic Pringle, PhD is a senior research scientist at Yale University’s Center for Emotional Intelligence. She is a regular contributor to Psychology Today and Creativity Post, and coeditor of The Cambridge Handbook of Creativity and Emotions and Crises, Creativity, and Innovation.

items.title: Massimo Pigliucci - How Evolution Shaped Consciousness, Emotion, & the Mind
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

What can evolution reveal about the developmental history of thinking and feeling? How did cognition and emotion (affect) co-develop over evolutionary time? Moreover, what can we learn from evolution about the mystery of consciousness?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Massimo Pigliucci is an American philosopher and biologist who is professor of philosophy at the City College of New York, former co-host of the Rationally Speaking Podcast, and former editor in chief for the online magazine Scientia Salon.
